div.h2: Maj(x, y, z) — Majority Vote Truth Table

p.code: (x AND y) XOR (x AND z) XOR (y AND z)

div.p: XOR ≡ OR for Maj — why? The three AND terms (x&y), (x&z), (y&z) are mutually exclusive in the "exactly 2 ones" case — at most one can be 1 at a time when the third input is 0. So XOR and OR agree. When all three inputs = 1, all three terms = 1:

Nonlinearity of Maj over GF(2) Maj contains pairwise AND terms: x·y, x·z, y·z. AND is multiplication in GF(2) — a nonlinear operation. A linear function satisfies: f(a⊕b) = f(a) ⊕ f(b) Test: Maj(1,0,0) ⊕ Maj(0,1,0) = 0 ⊕ 0 = 0 Maj(1⊕0, 0⊕1, 0) = Maj(1,1,0) = 1 ≠ 0 → Maj is nonlinear.
Row color key 🟩 Green: Maj = 1 (majority wins) 🟥 Red: Maj = 0 (minority wins) ★ marks the AND term that yielded 1
